description: Production-ready RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) system for travel technology support, featuring local-first deployment with cloud LLM fallback, comprehensive document ingestion, and intelligent query routing.

components.ingestionFramework: Typer CLI with Haystack document converters
components.observability: Loguru with rotating file handlers (Phoenix/Arize planned)

ingestion.batchProcessing: Adaptive batch sizing with retry logic
ingestion.metadataStorage: Source filename, page number, chunk offset

deploymentOptions.local: make install && make compose-infra && make run

strengths: Qdrant vector DB (fast HNSW indexing, scalable, supports filtering)
Proper chunking strategy (256 words, 64 overlap - good for Q&A)
Structured logging and configuration management (Pydantic Settings)
FastAPI for modern async API (automatic OpenAPI docs)
Pre-commit hooks and type checking (code quality)
Streaming responses (better UX for long answers)
criticalGaps: No evaluation framework (RAGAS/LangSmith) - Cannot measure RAG quality
Basic similarity search only - Missing hybrid search (semantic + BM25)
No reranking - Lower precision in top results
No document/query classification - Cannot route or filter intelligently
Missing URL metadata tracking - Cannot link back to original sources
No user feedback mechanism - Cannot improve based on likes/dislikes
No RBAC/filtering - All users see all documents
No chat history - Cannot handle multi-turn conversations
recommendedImprovements: Add RAGAS for automated evaluation (precision, recall, faithfulness)
Integrate Phoenix/Arize for request tracing and debugging
Implement hybrid search (BM25 + vector) using Qdrant payload filtering
Implement document classification pipeline (tags for filtering)
Extend metadata to include URLs, timestamps, document types
Add RBAC with Qdrant filtering by user role metadata
Consider Redis for caching frequent queries
Build TypeScript React component for frontend
Add PostgreSQL for chat history and user feedback storage
Upgrade embedding model to Qwen2.5-Coder or similar (better quality)

costBreakdown.infrastructure: Self-hosted or cloud VM ($10-50/month depending on specs)
costBreakdown.llmCosts: Zero (local Qwen 2.5 4B) or minimal Gemini API calls
costBreakdown.vectorDB: Qdrant OSS (free) or Qdrant Cloud ($25+/month for managed)

recommendations: Keep local llama.cpp for primary LLM (Qwen 2.5 7B for better quality)
Use Gemini Flash only for edge cases or complex queries (cost-efficient)
Self-host Qdrant in Docker for development, migrate to Qdrant Cloud for production
Use Phoenix OSS for observability (free, self-hosted)
Cache frequent queries in Redis to reduce LLM calls
Monitor token usage and implement rate limiting

phase1_foundation: Set up RAGAS evaluation framework with test queries
Integrate Phoenix for tracing and observability
Add URL metadata to ingestion pipeline
Implement document classification with tag extraction
phase2_quality: Upgrade embedding model to Qwen2.5-Coder or Voyage
Build comprehensive test suite with RAGAS metrics
phase3_production: Set up CI/CD pipeline with automated testing
phase4_optimization: Optimize chunk sizes based on evaluation results

forFutureClaudeRuns.codebaseContext: This is a production-grade RAG system for travel tech support. Prioritize: (1) Evaluation/testing before new features, (2) Observability for debugging, (3) Budget-friendly local-first architecture, (4) Modular design following Haystack patterns.
developmentGuidelines: Always read existing code before modifications - follow established patterns
Use Haystack components where possible (don't reinvent primitives)
Maintain backward compatibility with existing ingestion artifacts
Add type hints and docstrings for all new functions
Update tests when adding features (use pytest)
Run 'make format && make lint' before committing
Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones
Keep local-first approach - cloud services should be optional fallbacks
architecturalPrinciples: Modularity: Each service/component should have single responsibility
Configuration: All settings via environment variables or YAML
Observability: Log all critical operations with structured context
Error Handling: Graceful degradation, never crash on retrieval failures
Efficiency: Batch operations where possible, cache when beneficial
Privacy: Default to local models, minimize data sent to cloud
Testability: Write testable code, maintain high coverage

commonTasks.addNewDocumentSource: Extend ingestion/cli.py, add converter in pipelines/components.py, update filesystem.yaml config
commonTasks.changeChunkingStrategy: Modify ingestion config YAML (split_length, split_overlap, split_by)
commonTasks.addNewLLMProvider: Extend app/services/query_service.py, add settings in core/config.py, update .env.example
commonTasks.improveRetrieval: Modify app/services/qdrant_service.py for search logic, consider hybrid search implementation
commonTasks.addMetadataField: Update ingestion pipeline to populate field, modify Qdrant payload schema, extend API response model
commonTasks.setupEvaluation: Create tests/ directory, add RAGAS framework, build test query dataset, define metrics
commonTasks.addTracing: Integrate Phoenix SDK, wrap pipeline steps with spans, add context propagation

ingestion: ingestion/cli.py - CLI entry point for document processing

testingStrategy: Unit tests: Test individual components in isolation (embedding, chunking, etc.)
Integration tests: Test full ingestion pipeline and query flow
Evaluation tests: Use RAGAS to measure RAG quality (precision, recall, faithfulness)
Performance tests: Measure latency and throughput under load
Regression tests: Ensure changes don't degrade existing quality metrics
deploymentChecklist: Verify all required environment variables are set
Test health endpoints for all services (app, qdrant, llama servers)
Run ingestion on production document corpus
Validate retrieval quality with test queries
Set up monitoring and alerting (Phoenix, logs)
Configure backup strategy for Qdrant data
Set up CI/CD pipeline with automated tests
Document API endpoints and usage examples

knownIssues: Embedding batch size fallback may slow ingestion on large corpora
No conversation history - each query is independent
Reasoning filter is Qwen-specific - may need adjustment for other models
Local models require significant CPU/RAM (consider GPU deployment)
No automatic document refresh - ingestion must be triggered manually
futureConsiderations: Real-time document updates via webhook triggers
Answer citation with exact source highlighting
A/B testing framework for prompt optimization
Custom embedding fine-tuning on domain data
